brands.name: Baskin-Robbins
brands.notes: World's largest ice cream chain (Inspire Brands), ~976 US franchised units at end of 2024. Relatively low entry cost for a legacy QSR brand but combined royalty + ad fee of ~10.9% is high vs category norms.

riskFlags.title: High combined royalty + ad fee (~10.9%)
riskFlags.detail: 5.9% royalty plus 5.0% advertising fee totals ~10.9% of gross sales, notably higher than most QSR franchise burdens (typically 6-8%).

riskFlags.title: Franchisor litigation against ex-franchisee (Indiana)
riskFlags.detail: Baskin-Robbins (Inspire Brands) sued a former franchisee operating Broad Ripple and Greenwood, IN shops for continuing to operate as Baskin-Robbins after a June 2023 termination over ~$50,000 nonpayment, alleging trademark infringement and breach.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee fined for child labor violations
riskFlags.detail: A Utah operator of eight Baskin-Robbins stores was fined $49,833 by the U.S. Department of Labor for allowing 64 14- and 15-year-olds to work outside legal hours.

nationwide.note: Baskin-Robbins markets US franchise opportunities through the Inspire Brands franchising portal; brand operates ~976 US locations and continues to award territories nationally.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19: average annual gross sales across 844 franchised restaurants, FY ending Dec 29, 2024; median $521,177, range $11,670-$2,317,098.

totalUnits.note: US franchised units at end of 2024 (874 traditional + 102 non-traditional); 0 company-owned.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Liquid asset minimum; not stated in the Franchise Chatter FDD summary.

brands.name: Scooter's Coffee
brands.notes: Fast-growing drive-thru coffee kiosk franchise; ~900 stores across 32 states as of early 2026 with strong Item 19 AUV (~$877K). Higher capital requirement reflects real estate / drive-thru build-out.

riskFlags.title: Capital-intensive drive-thru kiosk model
riskFlags.detail: Total investment of roughly $894K-$1.39M driven by land/build-out for drive-thru kiosks; higher than typical coffee-shop entry cost.

nationwide.note: Development-areas map covers all 50 states + DC with available growth markets; franchisor states the compact drive-thru kiosk model can open in 'just about any market' (metro, suburban, rural). ~900 stores in 32 states as of Feb 2026.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19: average gross sales across 424 kiosk stores open the full 12 months ending Dec 31, 2023; median $869,610.

totalUnits.note: As of Dec 31, 2023: 729 franchised + 21 affiliate-owned. Brand reported ~900 stores in 32 states by Feb 2026.

brands.name: Marco's Pizza
brands.notes: Pizza franchise founded 1978 (franchising 1979); 1,200+ locations with prime US territories still available. Strong AUV but discloses bankruptcy/litigation in FDD and saw a multi-unit franchisee Chapter 11 in 2024.

riskFlags.title: Multi-unit franchisee Chapter 11 bankruptcy (Tampa Bay, 2024)
riskFlags.detail: Tampa Bay-area Marco's franchise owners (19 locations) filed for Chapter 11 in November 2024 to reorganize and sell four underperforming restaurants.

riskFlags.title: FDD discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy
riskFlags.detail: Marco's Pizza FDD discloses litigation and/or bankruptcy items, including renewal/system-standard disputes with franchisees, which prospective buyers should review.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / Lewitt Hackman franchise law analysis

nationwide.note: Official development site states '1,200+ locations and prime territories available in the U.S.' and accepts inquiries from all 50 states (plus Bahamas, Mexico, Puerto Rico); active US nationwide expansion incl. West Coast/California.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $285K-$804K per 2024 FDD review; a 2025 peersense summary cites $287K-$807K.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19-derived average gross sales (~$949K) per Franchise Chatter 2024 review; top quartile AUV $1,363,518 (FY2024). Figure represents systemwide franchised average.

totalUnits.note: 1,200+ locations across 35 states plus Puerto Rico, Bahamas, Mexico. 2025 FDD reported 997 franchised stores operating the full 2025 fiscal year.

brands.name: Dave's Hot Chicken
brands.notes: Explosive-growth Nashville-style hot chicken brand founded 2017 (franchising 2018); ~390 restaurants globally by mid-2026 with nearly 950 units in development. Acquired by Roark Capital in 2025 (~$1B). No Item 19 in FDD.

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 financial performance representation in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Dave's Hot Chicken does not disclose average unit sales in Item 19 of its FDD, so prospective franchisees have no franchisor-provided earnings figures; third-party estimates (~$2.45M AUV) are unofficial.

riskFlags.title: ADA website accessibility complaint (2025)
riskFlags.detail: A March 2025 complaint (Sandoval v. Dave's Hot Chicken Franchise Co., LLC) alleged the brand's website is not sufficiently digitally accessible.

riskFlags.title: Private-equity ownership change (Roark Capital, 2025)
riskFlags.detail: Dave's Hot Chicken was acquired by Roark Capital Group in 2025 in a deal valued near $1 billion; ownership/strategy changes are a consideration for prospective franchisees.

statesOpen.note: Listed among franchise-registration states; brand expanding aggressively in US.
Franchise-registration state where Dave's offers/sells franchises.
Franchise-registration state where Dave's offers/sells franchises.
Franchise-registration state where Dave's offers/sells franchises.
Franchise-registration state where Dave's offers/sells franchises.

yearFounded.note: First location opened May 2017 (LA parking-lot pop-up).

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range cited $620K-$1.96M (Sharpsheets); a vetmyfranchise 2025 FDD summary cites a wider $990K-$3.2M, likely format-dependent. Conflict noted.

totalInvestmentMax.note: See min note re: conflicting upper bound.

totalUnits.note: ~390 restaurants globally as of May 2026; nearly 950 units in development.

item19Available.note: FDD contains no Item 19 financial performance representation.

brands.name: 7 Brew Coffee
brands.notes: Drive-thru coffee stand brand founded 2017 in Arkansas, franchising since 2021; rapid growth to 500-700+ stands across ~35-38 states. Strong reported AUV (~$2M) but capital-intensive and tied to a modular-construction lawsuit between founders.

riskFlags.title: Founder/partner lawsuit over modular store builder (alleged $2M embezzlement)
riskFlags.detail: 7 Brew co-founder Ron Crume sued partners in Creative Modular Construction (the exclusive builder of 7 Brew's modular stores) alleging ~$2M was siphoned via unauthorized checks and commingled funds. Suit later dismissed without prejudice after resolution; control of CMC was restructured Jan 2026.
riskFlags.source: Springfield Daily Citizen / Mountain Home Observer

riskFlags.title: Separate investor/records dispute (Jimmy John's founder-linked entity)
riskFlags.detail: Stillwater (linked to the Jimmy John's founder) accused Drink House Holdings of failing to provide access to books and records and breaching an agreement, alleging it was a company member.

riskFlags.title: Capital-intensive drive-thru model with tiered escalating royalty
riskFlags.detail: Total investment ~$894K-$2.18M for a first traditional store; royalties are tiered starting at 4.5% and scaling up to 7% of weekly gross sales, plus 2% brand fund and 0.25% tech fee.

nationwide.note: Operating in ~35-38 states with active nationwide expansion; large multi-state development agreements (e.g., Flynn Group 160-unit, Jersey Mike's franchisee 70-unit Washington DC). Brand directs prospects to check territory availability via 7brew.com.

royaltyPercent.note: Tiered royalty starting at 4.5% of weekly gross sales, scaling up to 7%. Lower bound shown.

adFundPercent.note: 2% brand fund plus a separate 0.25% tech fee.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19-derived average annual gross sales per location (most recent FDD). An earlier 2023 FDD reported $2,397,892 AUV for 19 units open a full 12 months in 2022; figure reflects rapidly growing system.

isGrowing.note: Grew ~84% in units; from 38 stands (Jan 2023) to 500-700+ across ~35-38 states.
